rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Defendant Svi Corporation'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 14, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the depositions of defendant SVI Corporation¿s PMK and COR and production of documents was continued from May 25 at the parties¿ request. If all issues have not been resolved before then, by 4:30pm on June 13 the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, which issues remain in dispute and then appear at the hearing on June 14. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SHAW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Defendant Campbell Soup Company'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents; Requests For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 14, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel the depositions of defendant Campbell Soup Co.¿s person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and request for production of documents is denied. In light of the fact that defendant¿s PMK was recently deposed on some of the issues covered by the deposition notice and plaintiff¿s own deposition has not concluded, plaintiff has not provided good cause for its motion at the present time. Defendant¿s cross-motion for protective order is granted only to the extent that the motion seeks to defer the deposition sought by plaintiff until a reasonable time after the deposition of plaintiff has concluded. All requests for sanctions are denied. No party acted without substantial justification. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TOMMY MCCLAIN et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Defendant Campbell Soup Company'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents; Requests For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, June 14, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the depositions of defendant Campbell Soup Company¿s person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and request for production of documents is denied. In light of the fact that defendant¿s PMK was recently deposed on some of the issues covered by the deposition notice and neither of the living product identification witnesses listed in plaintiffs¿ standard interrogatory responses have been made available for deposition or their contact information provided to defendant, plaintiffs have not provided good cause for their motion at the present time. Defendant¿s cross-motion for protective order is granted only to the extent that the motion seeks to defer the deposition sought by plaintiffs until a reasonable time after plaintiffs have made Maurice Lawrence and Mel Swanson available for deposition or provided the contact information for Mr. Lawrence and Mr. Swanson to defendant. All requests for sanctions are denied. No party acted without substantial justification. = (220/HEK)
